name: Ovinnik
description: Ovinnik is a Slavic spirit or deity, most commonly considered a protective but also dangerous guardian of the threshing barn (ovin). The ovin was a crucial structure in Slavic villages, used for drying sheaves of grain before threshing. Ovinnik's role was to ensure the safe drying of the grain and prevent fires, which were a constant threat due to the open flames used for heating. He was often depicted as a small, fiery old man or a black cat, embodying both the warmth and the potential for destruction associated with fire. Ovinnik was believed to demand respect and offerings to ensure his benevolent protection. Failing to appease him could result in the ovin catching fire, leading to devastating losses for the community. Offerings typically included roosters, bliny (pancakes), or kasha (porridge). He was also believed to possess the ability to predict the future, particularly regarding the upcoming harvest. People would sometimes seek his guidance through rituals performed within the ovin. While primarily associated with the ovin, some accounts suggest Ovinnik held a broader influence over agricultural prosperity and the well-being of the harvest. His dual nature as both protector and potential destroyer reflects the precarious balance between human effort and the unpredictable forces of nature in the agricultural cycle. His power stemmed from his control over fire and the vital process of grain drying, making him a significant figure in the rural Slavic worldview.
subtitle: Guardian of the Threshing Barn

keyMyths.title: The Burning Ovin
keyMyths.description: A farmer neglects to give Ovinnik his due, and the ovin mysteriously bursts into flames, destroying the harvest. The farmer learns to respect the spirit and appease him in the future.

keyMyths.title: Ovinnik's Prophecy
keyMyths.description: A villager seeks Ovinnik's guidance to predict the harvest yield. Through a ritual involving grain and fire, Ovinnik reveals the coming abundance or scarcity.

keyMyths.title: The Black Cat's Warning
keyMyths.description: A black cat, believed to be an avatar of Ovinnik, warns a family of an impending fire in the ovin, allowing them to avert disaster.
